reasoning: The Treasury typically targets a lower TGA at the end of December (targeted $700B for 2024) compared to late October (~$800B). Furthermore, the debt limit suspension ends on January 1, 2025. After January 1, the Treasury will be unable to issue net new debt and will deploy extraordinary measures, drawing down the TGA to fund the government. Net cash flows in January typically do not support a massive increase in TGA without debt issuance. Thus, the TGA in late January 2025 should be significantly lower than its late October 2024 levels, making an increase highly unlikely.
